cue: Collect A's non-members of B, then B's non-members of A.
Ask whether every element of A is also in B.
Put the jewels in a Set, then count stones found in it.
prompt: Write `countJewels(jewels, stones)` returning how many characters of `stones` appear in `jewels`. Each character of `jewels` is distinct.
note: Building the jewel Set once turns each stone lookup into O(1), so the whole scan is O(|stones|) instead of O(|jewels|*|stones|).

cue: Emit A's uniques in A order, then B's uniques in B order.
prompt: Write `symmetricDiff(a, b)` returning the distinct values that appear in exactly one array: those only in `a` (in `a`'s order) followed by those only in `b` (in `b`'s order).
mapsTo: pattern behind LC 2215 · Find the Difference of Two Arrays

cue: Pour every value into one Set and read its size.
prompt: Write `distinctCount(lists)` taking an array of arrays and returning how many distinct values appear across all of them.
